Ad Text:
Milestone IT is partnering with a leading healthcare organisation to recruit an experienced Automation Engineer to join a high-impact Digital Strategy & Transformation team.

This is a hands-on opportunity to design and deliver innovative automation solutions across a large network, leveraging the Microsoft Power Platform to improve operational efficiency, reduce manual processes, and support better patient outcomes.

About the Role

Reporting to the Business Improvement and Automation Manager, you will play a key role in shaping and delivering scalable automation solutions across clinical and operational environments. You’ll work closely with stakeholders across the organisation to identify opportunities, design intelligent solutions, and drive digital transformation initiatives aligned with healthcare priorities.

Key Responsibilities Solution Development & Architecture
Design, develop, and deploy end-to-end solutions using Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, and Copilot Studio
Engage stakeholders to gather requirements and map business processes
Build scalable, secure, and maintainable automation frameworks
Integrate Power Platform solutions with Microsoft 365, SharePoint, and third-party systems
Develop API integrations using REST/HTTP services and custom connectors
Create data-driven dashboards and reporting solutions using Power BI
Design and implement AI-powered copilots for clinical and administrative use cases
Provide ongoing support and optimisation of deployed solutions
Application Lifecycle Management
Implement ALM practices including solution packaging and environment promotion
Configure CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps or GitHub
Manage version control and release processes
Stakeholder Engagement
Collaborate with clinical, operational, and IT teams to identify automation opportunities
Lead workshops, solution demonstrations, and training sessions
Governance & Compliance
Ensure solutions meet healthcare standards for data privacy, security, and compliance
Produce high-quality documentation including solution designs and user guides
Align with organisational governance, accessibility, and change management policies
Innovation & Continuous Improvement
Contribute to the organisation’s digital health strategy
Explore emerging technologies and promote best practices across the team
About You


Essential Skills & Experience
Strong hands-on experience with Power Apps (Canvas & Model-Driven) and Power Automate
Proven experience building solutions using Microsoft Copilot Studio
Experience across the Microsoft ecosystem: M365, SharePoint, Teams, Entra ID, Dataverse
Demonstrated API integration experience (REST APIs, HTTP actions, custom connectors)
Solid understanding of authentication methods (OAuth 2.0, API keys) and data formats (JSON, XML)
Ability to translate business and clinical needs into scalable technical solutions
Strong understanding of security, governance, and privacy in regulated environments
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Experience presenting technical solutions to both technical and non-technical audiences
Desirable
Experience working in healthcare or similarly regulated environments
Exposure to enterprise-scale digital transformation initiatives
Why Apply?
Work on meaningful projects that directly impact patient care and outcomes
Join a forward-thinking digital transformation team
Opportunity to shape automation standards and architecture at scale
Collaborative environment with strong executive engagement
If you’re passionate about automation, innovation, and making a real difference in healthcare, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now through Milestone IT.

HRD Employer of Choice, Accredited Family Inclusive Workplace

Join a forward-thinking organisation that values new ideas and innovation.

Data#3 is seeking a Delivery Focused Data & AI Architect to join our Professional Services team. You’ll lead solution design for modern data platforms on Microsoft Fabric and Azure, working directly with customers to define architecture, shape approaches, and guide decision making. Alongside architecture leadership, you’ll implement key solution components as part of project delivery—ensuring what we design is delivered to a high standard.

This is a hands on consulting role suited to someone who enjoys solving complex problems, shaping modern data approaches, and engaging with customers to deliver practical, secure and well governed data solutions. This role suits someone who is equally comfortable building solutions, advising clients, and mentoring delivery engineers.

In this role you will:

Lead solution architecture across Microsoft Fabric and Azure data platforms, including ingestion, transformation, modelling, governance and analytics

Design and implement data pipelines, lakehouse architectures, orchestration patterns and platform configuration

Embed security, governance, lineage and data quality by design across all solutions

Produce high-quality architecture artefacts including HLDs, LLDs, data models, diagrams and decision records

Run discovery, technical assessments and facilitate customer workshops

Guide customers through solution options, risks, trade-offs and implementation approaches

Support pre-sales with solution input, estimates and technical guidance when required

About You

5–8+ years’ experience in data engineering, data architecture or analytics solution design

Strong consulting capability with experience delivering end-to-end data platforms

Hands-on experience designing, configuring and deploying Microsoft Fabric and Azure data components

Proven ability to implement governance, lineage, data quality and security-by-design

Excellent communication skills with the ability to simplify complexity and influence decisions

Relevant certifications (DP-600, DP-700, DP-900) or willingness to obtain

Technical Skills:

Microsoft Fabric and Azure data platforms, including lakehouse architectures, pipelines, analytics and governance

Modern data architecture patterns (medallion/lakehouse, ETL/ELT, analytical vs transactional workloads)

Data governance and quality frameworks aligned to enterprise and regulatory standards

Deployment, automation and environment management practices

Azure AI and Foundry services, including GenAI models, embeddings, search and document intelligence

Applied AI patterns, including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and enterprise grounding approaches


Who is Data#3?

Data#3 is ASX200 listed and reported gross sales of $3 billion in FY25. Headquartered in Brisbane we have approximately 1,400 employees across 12 locations in Australia and Fiji.

We have been voted HRD's Employer of Choice for the last 10 years and named one of Australia’s Best Workplaces in Technology by Great Place To Work® in 2024

At Data#3, we value diversity, equity, and inclusion as key to a thriving workplace. We are committed to building a culture where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued—and we encourage your application.

Data#3 will request as part of the recruitment process, reference checks, police checks and verification of your right to work in Australia.

Ad Text:
My client a Federal Government Body is seeking an ITSM Automation Specialist to join their growing team. This is a 6 month contract with a likelihood of extending based Sydney CBD.

This role is focused on automating and integrating IT service management processes using the Microsoft ecosystem to improve efficiency, service quality, and user experience.

The ITSM Automation Specialist plays a pivotal role in modernising The Organisation’s service management ecosystem through the design, integration, and continuous enhancement of intelligent automation across the new HaloITSM platform and connected platforms and services.
  
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS
Essential

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or equivalent experience.
3–5 years’ experience in automation design, service orchestration, or systems integration within enterprise environments.
Hands-on experience with HaloITSM, Power Automate, Logic Apps, Azure Automation, or equivalent workflow automation technologies.
Familiarity with API development, webhooks, and scripting languages (e.g. PowerShell, Python) for system integration and task automation.
Strong understanding of Microsoft 365, Azure, identity and access management (Entra ID), and HaloITSM platform workflows.
Experience implementing or enhancing service catalogue, self-service, and workflow automation features.
Microsoft Certified - Power Platform Developer Associate or Power Platform Functional Consultant Associate.

ITIL 4 Foundation.
  
Desirable

Experience with AI-assisted automation or intelligent workflow tools (e.g. Copilot, Power Automate AI Builder, Azure OpenAI).
Exposure to service analytics, ticket classification AI, or predictive automation in IT operations.
Familiarity with integration middleware (e.g. Azure Service Bus, API Management, Event Grid) to support orchestration and interoperability.
Experience supporting automation governance frameworks within DevSecOps environments.
Experience working in regulated or government environments with data residency and compliance considerations.

About the role:

The Platform Security Engineer is a valued member of our engineering team. We value evidence-backed opinions and offer the opportunity to play an integral part in shaping engineering rhythm, culture, and processes across planning, architecture and design, coding, deployment, and support.


In an ever-changing environment, Lendi Group is leading the charge in developing industry-first technology designed to offer Australian homeowners transparency, simplicity, and convenience in their home loan experience.
The Platform Security Engineer partners with engineering teams to evaluate planned changes, minimise security risk, and continuously improve our security posture. A key focus of the role is driving vulnerability and security findings down over time, improving security standards, and embedding secure-by-default practices across both traditional and AI-enabled systems.

Please note: this role will be a 12 Month Fixed Term Contract

Job Description


Working closely with the Cyber Security team to ensure security principles are consistently applied across the engineering environment.
Collaborating and coding with engineering teams to uplift secure development practices and reduce recurring security findings.
Designing, building, and maintaining secure cloud and microservice solutions.
Improving security posture through effective use of COTS products, platform controls, and network infrastructure.
Vulnerability and Threat Management — monitoring and assessing assets including cloud infrastructure, containers, packages, APIs, and requests, with a focus on remediation and measurable risk reduction.
Supporting the secure design and operation of AI-enabled systems, including LLM integrations, automation agents, and MCP-based architectures.
Implementing and reviewing AI security guardrails such as access controls, data protection, prompt-injection mitigation, and output validation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams including engineering, operations, compliance, data, and AI platform teams.
Contributing as an individual contributor within a cross-functional DevOps / SecOps team.
Build or integrate services to improve security and compliance

Qualifications


Strong understanding of networking, infrastructure, and applications from a DevOps perspective with a security focus.
Hands-on experience implementing and maintaining security controls across cloud environments (particularly AWS) and access management solutions (e.g. Auth0).
Experience with security logging, monitoring, and vulnerability assessment, with a demonstrated ability to drive findings down and improve baseline security standards.
Passion for secure coding practices and identifying common implementation and operational security risks.

Desirable Experience

AWS technologies such as CloudFormation, Terraform, IAM, WAF, Node.js and/or TypeScript.
Docker, containerised environments, serverless architectures, and Kubernetes.
Event-driven systems.
Securing AI platforms, including LLM integrations, MCP implementations, AI guardrails, and automation/build agents.

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
An additional week’s Loyalty Leave each year after reaching 3 years’ service.
Wellness initiatives with a strong focus on psychological safety.

We’re committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive community at Lendi Group. We believe that a team reflecting the world around us leads to greater innovation, stronger collaboration, and a more engaging workplace.

Our culture is guided by our 3 core values: We Are Stronger United; Act Like You Own It; and Keep Home Loans Human. Our values are part of our core DNA that helps Lendi Group to attract, engage and evolve the right talent and build best-in-class products.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing, purpose-driven company that’s transforming the homeownership journey.

Ready to contribute to Lendi Group’s next chapter? Apply now and be part of something big!

As a Data Professional in ASIS, you will have the chance to actively enable our operational teams to make the best use of ASIS’s unique data assets.

Software Engineers in ASIS play a pivotal role in enabling us to achieve our unique mission and drive real world outcomes. They design, build and maintain innovative bespoke systems that maximise the value and impact of our data. They are highly motivated and engage in cross-functional collaboration, working closely with other engineers, data scientists, and end-users to translate business needs into scalable, high-impact solutions, ensuring seamless integration across the entire stack.

Key Responsibilities and Tasks

Engage in cross-functional collaboration with other engineers, data scientists, and end-users, to design, develop, and maintain innovative bespoke systems that maximise the value and impact of ASIS’s data
Enable high value mission outcomes by building engaging and intuitive UX for end-users, robust backend APIs for engineers and data scientists, or solutions that bridge both
Write clean, maintainable, and scalable code, actively participate in code reviews, and contribute to fostering a culture of continuous improvement
Design, implement or apply efficient data structures and algorithms to optimize performance
Continuously learn and develop new skills, taking ownership of your personal and professional growth, while working independently with minimal supervision
Clearly communicate business and technical concepts to diverse range of stakeholders, from end-users to senior leadership.

Education, Qualification and Experience Requirements

The following education, qualifications and/or experience will be highly regarded:

Formal training, such as a bachelor’s degree in a relevant field such as computer science, software engineering, or equivalent
Experience working and succeeding as a software engineer in industry, government, intelligence or research institutions
Demonstrated independent drive to succeed in a small, tight-knit team
Excellent verbal and written communication skills

To apply, please visit the ASIS website and submit an application through the Talent Register - Data Engineering Professionals.

Category:
Engineering - Software (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Are you keen to design and develop world-class capabilities, turning data into knowledge and operational outcomes? Are you passionate about building solutions to allow data to tell its story? ASIS offers an opportunity to achieve these goals in an uncompromisingly unique environment. As a Data Professional in ASIS, you will have the chance to actively enable our operational teams to make the best use of ASIS’s unique data assets.

We are looking for motivated and experienced Data Engineers to join our team and tackle unique data challenges that directly impact our business operations.

This role is perfect for candidates with several years’ experience in data engineering, data integration, and data management. Using tools like Python, NiFi and Java, you will be responsible for designing, building, operationalising and securing data pipelines, stores and real-time processing systems. You will leverage your experience to help inform the design of our next generation of scalable, secure, reliable and performant data systems.

ASIS operates in an uncompromisingly unique environment. No two days, or datasets, will be the same as you maximise the use of ASIS’s operational data holdings—a challenge that sets us apart from the ordinary.

Key Responsibilities and Tasks

Support the design, development and maintenance of our data ingest pipelines
Troubleshoot issues related to data ingestion, identifying root causes and implementing sustainable solutions promptly
Support the design, development, delivery and maintenance of large-scale secure data systems
Collaborate closely with data colleagues, analysts and mission areas to understand data requirements and support data delivery initiatives
Contribute to communicating and documenting data workflows, processes, and solutions for knowledge sharing

Education, Qualification and Experience Requirements

The following education, qualifications and/or experience will be highly regarded:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field
Basic understanding of programming concepts; familiarity with Python, Java, or similar languages is a plus
Strong interest in learning about data management, ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes, and data pipelines
Have a knowledge of database management systems (relational, and non-relational) and bulk data concepts
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and willingness to learn from experienced professionals
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail

To apply, please visit the ASIS website and submit an application through the Talent Register - Data Engineering Professionals.

Are you keen to design and develop world-class capabilities, turning data into knowledge and operational outcomes? Are you passionate about building solutions to allow data to tell its story? ASIS offers an opportunity to achieve these goals in an uncompromisingly unique environment. As a Data Professional in ASIS, you will have the chance to actively enable our operational teams to make the best use of ASIS’s unique data assets.

We are looking for motivated entry-level Data Technicians to join our team and tackle unique data challenges that directly impact our business operations. This role is perfect for candidates eager to kick-start their career in data engineering, integration, and management. You will be responsible for loading, running and monitoring data pipelines, including associated Standard Operating Procedures and administration. You will be using tools like Python to ensure seamless data ingestion into our systems, and take pride in building and maintaining an efficient and critical capability. ASIS operates in an uncompromisingly unique environment. No two days, or datasets, will be the same as you maximise the use of ASIS’s operational data holdings—a challenge that sets us apart from the ordinary.

Key Responsibilities and Tasks

Learn under the guidance of senior team members, and then take ownership of data ingestion pipelines
Implement basic data validation and quality checks to ensure data integrity throughout the ingestion process
Troubleshoot issues related to data ingestion, and assist with the implementation of sustainable solutions promptly
Collaborate closely with data colleagues to support delivery initiatives
Contribute to documenting and communicating data workflows, processes, and solutions for knowledge sharing

Education, Qualification and Experience Requirements

The following education, qualifications and/or experience will be highly regarded:

Tafe Certificate III/IV in Information Technology or related field; or strong performance in online Programming or Data courses
Basic understanding of programming concepts; familiarity with Python or similar languages is a plus
Strong interest in learning about data management, ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes, data pipelines, and implementing a robust data program
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and willingness to learn from experienced professionals
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail

To apply, please visit the ASIS website and submit an application through the Talent Register - Data Engineering Professionals.

Category:
Networks & Systems Administration (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Systems Engineers in ASIS maintain highly specialised systems delivering to operational teams around the globe. We have a large number of systems delivering a variety of services at a range of maturities. Extending beyond a normal systems administration role, there is never a dull moment and we work closely with our data scientists and analytical teams to have a real impact.

Key Responsibilities and Tasks

Build, maintain and support development, test and production environments including CI/CD
Implement and maintain best-practice security controls, including both on-system and throughout the development lifecycle
Develop and maintain high-quality documentation including logical, system and network design documentation
Perform analysis of monitoring, error reporting, alerting and issue reports to find the root cause of problems
Collaborate with other engineers and data scientists to implement robust and scalable software solutions in a problem space like no other
Contribute to the design and development of ground-breaking solutions for unique data challenges across the ASIS mission
Independently learn and develop new skills and techniques with an eye to practical applications
Communicate regularly and pre-emptively with peers and colleagues to keep everyone informed

Education, Qualification and Experience Requirements

The following education, qualifications and/or experience will be highly regarded

Formal training, such as a bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or related field
Experience in designing and maintaining large scale storage systems (Gluster, Ceph, etc)
Strong containerisation experience using Docker, Podman and Kubernetes
Experience in distributed computing technologies particularly Hadoop, Elasticsearch, Solr
Strong experience in designing and tuning high-performance compute and AI infrastructure

To apply, please visit the ASIS website and submit an application through the Talent Register - Data Engineering Professionals.

Join Us at a Defining Moment

At Intelligen, we’re building something different. Over the past three years, we’ve evolved from a bold startup to a trusted partner for some of Australia’s most complex data transformations. We’ve helped organisations move from legacy to modern platforms, embedded governance into decision-making, and brought AI into the hands of the business, responsibly and at speed.

But we’re just getting started. As we scale into our next phase of growth, we’re looking for senior team members who don’t just want to deliver great solutions, but help shape the future of data and AI capability across the Asia-Pacific region.

We’re now expanding our presence across APAC, and we’re looking for an experienced AI Architect to help lead the design and delivery of modern AI and data platforms across the region.

About the Role

We’re looking for an AI Architect, based in Sydney, who can work across APAC clients to design and deliver scalable, secure, and business-aligned AI solutions built on the Microsoft ecosystem, with a strong focus on Microsoft Fabric.

You’ll bring deep architecture capability across data, analytics, and AI, along with strong consulting skills and stakeholder engagement experience. You will work closely with business leaders, technology teams, data practitioners, and delivery squads to shape modern platform strategies and architect end-to-end solutions that enable trusted, production-ready AI.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in an agile environment, can confidently work across cultures and time zones, and is excited by the opportunity to help scale something from the ground up.

What You’ll Do
Lead the architecture and design of AI and data solutions for 1-2 clients across Australia (Microsoft ecosystem)
Design modern, scalable solutions using Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Services, Power BI, Microsoft Purview, and the broader Microsoft AI stack
Define end-to-end architecture patterns across data ingestion, transformation, semantic modelling, governance, machine learning, and AI enablement
Support the design of enterprise AI use cases including predictive analytics, generative AI, copilots, and intelligent automation
Translate business and functional requirements into well-structured, secure, and scalable architecture designs
Guide clients on platform modernisation and migration strategies into Microsoft Fabric and Azure-native environments
Establish architecture guardrails, design standards, and best practices across performance, security, governance, and operational support
Collaborate with engineering and consulting teams to ensure solutions are delivered in line with architecture intent and business outcomes
Support data governance, lineage, quality, and compliance initiatives through Microsoft-centric capabilities such as Purview and Fabric
Contribute to project planning, solution design, and delivery execution across a range of client engagements
Support pre-sales, solution shaping, proposal development, and client workshops as we continue to grow our APAC footprint



Requirements


7+ years’ experience in data, analytics, AI, or architecture roles, with strong consulting or client-facing exposure
Proven experience designing enterprise data and AI solutions on Microsoft Azure and Microsoft Fabric
Strong understanding of Fabric capabilities including Data Factory, Data Engineering, Data Science, Data Warehouse, Real-Time Intelligence, OneLake, and Power BI
Experience architecting modern AI solutions using Azure AI services, Azure Machine Learning, Azure OpenAI, or Microsoft Copilot-related patterns
Strong understanding of data architecture principles including medallion design, semantic modelling, master data, metadata, lineage, and governance
Experience with Microsoft Purview and enterprise data governance practices is highly regarded
Strong knowledge of cloud security, access models, privacy, and responsible AI considerations within Microsoft environments
Ability to engage with senior stakeholders and translate strategy into practical architecture and delivery plans
Excellent communication, written and spoken
Ability to work independently, flexibly, and collaboratively across distributed teams
A mindset of curiosity, delivery excellence, and continuous learning



Benefits



We’re not just delivering AI and data projects.. we’re humanising them. That means we care deeply about the how, not just the what. We value curiosity, creativity, and a willingness to challenge the status quo. We look for people who are driven to build a business, get curious, and offer opinions and ideas.

You’ll join a team that’s smart, kind, and ambitious. You’ll have real influence - in your projects, your practice, and the broader business. And as we grow, so will you. Here’s what that looks like:

Grow With Purpose

Structured development pathways so you always know what you’re working toward.
Paid certifications across all major cloud and data technologies (mastery matters)
Weekly training and knowledge exchanges where practitioners share real project insights.


Support That Helps You Thrive

A dedicated support lead to mentor, guide and help you navigate your career
Regular 360° feedback loops, giving you clear direction and honest insight into your progress.


A Community You’ll Enjoy Being Part Of

Innovation days where you can experiment, prototype and sharpen new skills.
Team days, social catchups,  many new community clubs…
Opportunities to lead, influence and shape our growing practice as we expand across the region.


If you want to be part of a team that cares just as much about how we deliver as what we deliver; where curiosity is valued, ideas matter, and your impact is felt - drop us a message and start the conversation today.

Initial contract duration
12 months

Extension term
12 months

Location of work

QLD, WA, ACT, VIC, NSW, NT, SA, TAS

Candidates must be an Australian Citizen

Job details


As a LIMS Developer, you will contribute towards the LIMS Simplification Project by developing new macros, configuring software, testing, troubleshooting, and producing technical documentation. You will work as part of a team of developers and subject matter experts drawn from across the Department, and liaise with external LIMS consultants where required.
You will also be involved in conducting demonstrations and gathering requirements with key stakeholders.
This position reports to the Senior LIMS Developer.

Key duties and responsibilities


Participate in requirements gathering and solution design workshops with stakeholders and external consultants.

Provide technical inputs in solution design.
Work with the Departments development team and LIMS vendor to configure new LIMS system within the Department.
Code new functionality as part of the new LIMS development.
Perform testing of new LIMS system as required.
Conduct technical documentation.
Maintain best software engineering (coding) practice.
Provide support for end user training.
Participate in user acceptance testing activities such as scenario gathering, configuration and scenario mapping, script preparation, bug fixing, and unit testing.
Work with LIMS Administrators for BAU support and implementing enhancements.
Promote a culture of safety and a safe working environment, ensuring adherence to health and safety protocols.
Desirable Experience: Participate and lead requirements gathering and solution design workshops with stakeholders and external consultants.
Provide expert advice in business improvement and process improvement strategies.
Guide and influence the developers' team for optimal solution design.
Assist key stakeholders, project managers and lead developer in strategic direction of the LIMS implementation project.
Work with project managers and lead developer for project planning and resource forecasting.
Lead and mentor developers and Subject Matter Experts from Laboratories in implementing LIMS software.
Work with the development team and LIMS vendor to configure new LIMS system within Department.
Code new functionality as part of the new LIMS development.
Perform testing of new LIMS system as required.
Conduct and review technical documentation.
Maintain and improve best software engineering (coding) practice.
Provide support for end user training.
Participate in user acceptance testing activities such as scenario gathering, configuration and scenario mapping, script preparation, bug fixing, and unit testing.
Work with LIMS Administrators for BAU support and implementing enhancements.
Upskill and share knowledge of proprietary LIMS scripting language.
Promote a culture of safety and a safe working environment, ensuring adherence to health and safety protocols.
Tertiary qualifications in Science or Computer Science.
Demonstrated experience in the use of computer-based data management systems.
Experience working in a scientific analytical laboratory or experience with commercial LIMS systems will be looked on favourably.
Demonstrated high level of commitment, self-motivation and initiative, ability to work effectively in a team environment and to exercise sound judgement in a competitive and changing environment. Good organisational skills and ability to prioritise workflows.

To be considered for the role click the 'apply' button or for more information about this and other opportunities please contact Muhammad Warsi on (0)2 6151 9201 or email: mwarsi@paxus.com.au and quote the above job reference number.
